headline_drivers: Q1 FY26 earnings beat with strong growth: revenue up ~17–18% YoY and ~5% QoQ, driving a ~5% stock move on the print and setting up a positive revision cycle (earnings release and commentary, early May 2026)

Analyst upgrade and target hike: Lake Street raises price target to $54 from $44 and reiterates Buy, citing Q4 and ongoing revenue outperformance and improved outlook for Measurement Systems, reinforcing the earnings-driven rerating (broker note referenced in Intellectia.ai, 2026)

Continued momentum in precision measurement/industrial automation theme, with technical breakout above prior resistance and high RSI/volume confirming a momentum chase in a small/mid-cap name (volume spike and top-1d gappers screens, 2026-05-12)
summary: The move in VPG is primarily being driven by a sharp fundamental re-rating after a strong start to FY26: management reported roughly mid‑teens to high‑teens year‑over‑year revenue growth and sequential acceleration, beating expectations and highlighting strength in its Measurement Systems division and cost-savings leverage. That earnings strength has been reinforced by a Lake Street price-target hike and Buy rating, which validates the improving trajectory and encourages fresh institutional interest in what is still a relatively small-cap, thinly held name. Once the stock broke through prior resistance levels in the low/mid‑60s, elevated volume (4–5x normal) and a very high RSI show that technical and momentum buyers have piled in, amplifying the move beyond what the earnings print alone would normally justify. There is no evidence of a single big-ticket customer win or regulatory shock; this looks like an earnings-beat-and-recovery story that has tipped into a momentum breakout in a low-float industrial/precision-measurement name.
